criteria.label: Compute D_KL(P || Q) by hand for a small discrete distribution (2–4 outcomes).
criteria.example: For p = [0.5, 0.5], q = [0.9, 0.1], compute 0.5 log(0.5/0.9) + 0.5 log(0.5/0.1) in chosen log base and report the numeric value.

criteria.label: Verify D_KL(P || Q) = H(P, Q) − H(P) with NumPy on the same PMFs.
criteria.example: Implement both formulas for p = [0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25], q = [0.4, 0.3, 0.2, 0.1] and assert they match within 1e-10.

criteria.label: Explain why KL is asymmetric and give a numeric counterexample.
criteria.example: Show D_KL(P||Q) ≠ D_KL(Q||P) for the fair vs biased coin pair and state which direction is larger.

criteria.label: Connect cross-entropy classification loss to minimizing KL from labels to the model.
criteria.example: Write H(P, Q) = H(P) + D_KL(P||Q) for one-hot P and argue why only the KL term affects gradient updates during training.
